paper_metadata.title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners
paper_metadata.abstract:
This paper explores a simple method for improving the zero-shot learning abilities of language models. We show that instruction tuning -- finetuning language models on a collection of tasks described via instructions -- substantially improves zero-shot performance on unseen tasks.
  We take a 137B parameter pretrained language model and instruction-tune it on over 60 NLP tasks verbalized via natural language instruction templates. We evaluate this instruction-tuned model, which we call FLAN, on unseen task types. FLAN substantially improves the performance of its unmodified counterpart and surpasses zero-shot 175B GPT-3 on 20 of 25 tasks that we evaluate. FLAN even outperforms few-shot GPT-3 by a large margin on ANLI, RTE, BoolQ, AI2-ARC, OpenbookQA, and StoryCloze. Ablation studies reveal that number of finetuning datasets, model scale, and natural language instructions are key to the success of instruction tuning.

extraction_data.innovation: FLAN is a large-scale language model trained to perform zero-shot tasks across multiple domains without task-specific fine-tuning. It uses a Transformer architecture and demonstrates strong performance on a wide range of tasks.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (aggregated from 62 NLP datasets)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised discriminative fine-tuning using instruction templates
extraction_data.optimizer: Adam (as per prior work; not explicitly mentioned in this paper)
extraction_data.hardware_used: NVIDIA V100 GPUs (as inferred from typical large-scale model training)
extraction_data.extension: The model uses a Transformer architecture with a novel training objective that enables zero-shot performance across diverse tasks without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving zero-shot learning abilities of large language models.
extraction_data.application: Zero-shot performance on unseen NLP tasks (e.g., natural language inference, reading comprehension, sentiment analysis, etc.)
extraction_data.paper_title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners

extraction_data.innovation: Finetuning large language models with instructions to enable zero-shot performance on unseen tasks.
extraction_data.application: General language tasks, question answering, text classification
